title: Processing audio data to compensate for partial hearing loss or an adverse hearing environment
abstract: Methods a provided for improving an audio scene for people suffering from hearing loss or for adverse hearing environments. Audio objects may be prioritized. In some implementations, audio objects that correspond to dialogue may be assigned to a highest priority level. Other implementations may involve assigning the highest priority to other types of audio objects, such as audio objects that correspond to events. During a process of dynamic range compression, higher-priority objects may be boosted more, or cut less, than lower-priority objects. Some lower-priority audio objects may fall below the threshold of human hearing, in which case the audio objects may be dropped and not rendered.
claims:
Claims 1. A method, comprising: receiving audio data comprising a plurality of audio objects, the audio objects including audio signals and associated audio object metadata, the audio object metadata including audio object position metadata; receiving reproduction environment data comprising an indication of a number of reproduction speakers in a reproduction environment; determining at least one audio object type from among a list of audio object types that includes dialogue; making an audio object prioritization based, at least in part, on the audio object type, wherein making the audio object prioritization involves assigning a highest priority to audio objects that correspond to dialogue; adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization; and rendering the audio objects into a plurality of speaker feed signals based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata, wherein each speaker feed signal corresponds to at least one of the reproduction speakers within the reproduction environment. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising receiving hearing environment data comprising at least one factor selected from a group of factors consisting of: a model of hearing loss; a deficiency of at least one reproduction speaker; and current environmental noise, wherein adjusting the audio object levels is based, at least in part, on the hearing environment data. 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein rendering involves rendering the audio objects to locations in a virtual acoustic space. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the rendering involves increasing a distance between at least some audio objects in the virtual acoustic space. 5. The method of claim 3 or claim 4, wherein the virtual acoustic space includes a front area and a back area and wherein the rendering involves increasing a distance between at least some audio objects in the front area of the virtual acoustic space. 6. The method of any one of claims 3-5, wherein the rendering involves rendering the audio objects according to a plurality of virtual speaker locations within the virtual acoustic space. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the audio object metadata includes metadata indicating audio object size and wherein making the audio object prioritization involves applying a function that reduces a priority of non-dialogue audio objects according to increases in audio object size. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, further comprising selecting at least one audio object that will not be rendered based, at least in part, on the audio object prioritization. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein adjusting the audio object levels involves differentially adjusting levels in frequency bands of corresponding audio signals. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the audio object metadata includes audio object prioritization metadata and wherein adjusting the audio object levels is based, at least in part, on the audio object prioritization metadata. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, further comprising: determining that an audio object has audio signals that include a directional component and a diffuse component; and reducing a level of the diffuse component. 12. The method of any one of claims 1-11, wherein adjusting the audio object levels involves dynamic range compression. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the reproduction environment comprises a Dolby Surround 5.1 configuration, a Dolby Surround 7.1 configuration, a Hamasaki 22.2 surround sound configuration, a headphone configuration, a Dolby Surround 5.1.2 configuration, a Dolby Surround 7.1.2 configuration or a Dolby Atmos configuration. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the audio object metadata includes audio object type metadata and wherein determining the audio object type involves evaluating the object type metadata. 15. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein determining the audio object type involves analyzing the audio signals of audio objects. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the list of audio object types also includes background music, events and ambiance. 17. A method, comprising: receiving audio data comprising a plurality of audio objects, the audio objects including audio signals and associated audio object metadata; extracting one or more features from the audio data; determining an audio object type based, at least in part, on features extracted from the audio signals, wherein the audio object type is selected from a list of audio object types that includes dialogue; making an audio object prioritization based, at least in part, on the audio object type, wherein the audio object prioritization determines, at least in part, a gain to be applied during a process of rendering the audio objects into speaker feed signals and wherein making the audio object prioritization involves assigning a highest priority to audio objects that correspond to dialogue; and adding audio object prioritization metadata, based on the audio object prioritization, to the audio object metadata. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the list of audio object types also includes background music, events and ambiance. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the one or more features include at least one feature from a list of features consisting of: spectral flux; loudness; audio object size; entropy-related features; harmonicity features; spectral envelope features; phase features; and temporal features. 20. The method of any one of claims 17-19, further comprising: determining a confidence score regarding each audio object type determination; and applying a weight to each confidence score to produce a weighted confidence score, the weight corresponding to the audio object type determination, wherein making an audio object prioritization is based, at least in part, on the weighted confidence score. 21. The method of any one of claims 17-20, wherein determining the audio object type involves a machine learning method. 22. The method of any one of claims 17-21, further comprising: receiving hearing environment data comprising a model of hearing loss; adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization and the hearing environment data; and rendering the audio objects into a plurality of speaker feed signals based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata, wherein each speaker feed signal corresponds to at least one of the reproduction speakers within the reproduction environment. 23. The method of any one of claims 17-21, wherein the audio object metadata includes audio object size metadata and wherein the audio object position metadata indicates locations in a virtual acoustic space, further comprising: receiving hearing environment data comprising a model of hearing loss; receiving indications of a plurality of virtual speaker locations within the virtual acoustic space; adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization and the hearing environment data; and rendering the audio objects to the plurality of virtual speaker locations within the virtual acoustic space based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata and the audio object size metadata. 24. An apparatus, comprising: means for receiving audio data comprising a plurality of audio objects, the audio objects including audio signals and associated audio object metadata, the audio object metadata including at least audio object position metadata; means for receiving reproduction environment data comprising an indication of a number of reproduction speakers in a reproduction environment; means for determining at least one audio object type from among a list of audio object types that includes dialogue; means for making an audio object prioritization based, at least in part, on the audio object type, wherein making the audio object prioritization involves assigning a highest priority to audio objects that correspond to dialogue; 

 means for adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization; and means for rendering the audio objects into a plurality of speaker feed signals based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata, wherein each speaker feed signal corresponds to at least one of the reproduction speakers within the reproduction environment. 25. The apparatus of claim 24, wherein the means for receiving audio data includes means for receiving hearing environment data comprising at least one factor selected from a group of factors consisting of: a model of hearing loss; a deficiency of at least one reproduction speaker; and current environmental noise, wherein adjusting the audio object levels is based, at least in part, on the hearing environment data. 26. An apparatus, comprising: an interface system capable of receiving audio data comprising a plurality of audio objects, the audio objects including audio signals and associated audio object metadata, the audio object metadata including at least audio object position metadata; and a control system capable of: receiving reproduction environment data comprising an indication of a number of reproduction speakers in a reproduction environment; determining at least one audio object type from among a list of audio object types that includes dialogue; making an audio object prioritization based, at least in part, on the audio object type, wherein making the audio object prioritization involves assigning a highest priority to audio objects that correspond to dialogue; adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization; and rendering the audio objects into a plurality of speaker feed signals based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata, wherein each speaker feed signal corresponds to at least one of the reproduction speakers within the reproduction environment. 27. The apparatus of claim 26, wherein the interface system is capable of receiving hearing environment data comprising at least one factor selected from a group of factors consisting of: a model of hearing loss; a deficiency of at least one reproduction speaker; and current environmental noise, and wherein the control system is capable of adjusting the audio object levels based, at least in part, on the hearing environment data. 28. A non-transitory medium having software stored thereon, the software including instructions for controlling at least one device for: receiving audio data comprising a plurality of audio objects, the audio objects including audio signals and associated audio object metadata, the audio object metadata including at least audio object position metadata; receiving reproduction environment data comprising an indication of a number of reproduction speakers in a reproduction environment; determining at least one audio object type from among a list of audio object types that includes dialogue; making an audio object prioritization based, at least in part, on the audio object type, wherein making the audio object prioritization involves assigning a highest priority to audio objects that correspond to dialogue; adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization; and rendering the audio objects into a plurality of speaker feed signals based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata, wherein each speaker feed signal corresponds to at least one of the reproduction speakers within the reproduction environment. 29. The non-transitory medium of claim 28, wherein the software includes instructions for controlling the at least one device to receive hearing environment data comprising at least one factor selected from a group of factors consisting of: a model of hearing loss; a deficiency of at least one reproduction speaker; and current environmental noise, and wherein adjusting the audio object levels is based, at least in part, on the hearing environment data. 30. An apparatus, comprising: means for receiving audio data comprising a plurality of audio objects, the audio objects including audio signals and associated audio object metadata; means for extracting one or more features from the audio data; means for determining an audio object type based, at least in part, on features extracted from the audio signals, wherein the audio object type is selected from a list of audio object types that includes dialogue; means for making an audio object prioritization based, at least in part, on the audio object type, wherein the audio object prioritization determines, at least in part, a gain to be applied during a process of rendering the audio objects into speaker feed signals and wherein 

 making the audio object prioritization involves assigning a highest priority to audio objects that correspond to dialogue; and means for adding audio object prioritization metadata, based on the audio object prioritization, to the audio object metadata. 31. The apparatus of claim 30, further comprising: means for receiving hearing environment data comprising a model of hearing loss; means for adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization and the hearing environment data; and means for rendering the audio objects into a plurality of speaker feed signals based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata, wherein each speaker feed signal corresponds to at least one of the reproduction speakers within the reproduction environment. 32. An apparatus, comprising: an interface system capable of receiving audio data comprising a plurality of audio objects, the audio objects including audio signals and associated audio object metadata; and a control system capable of: extracting one or more features from the audio data; determining an audio object type based, at least in part, on features extracted from the audio signals, wherein the audio object type is selected from a list of audio object types that includes dialogue; making an audio object prioritization based, at least in part, on the audio object type, wherein the audio object prioritization determines, at least in part, a gain to be applied during a process of rendering the audio objects into speaker feed signals and wherein making the audio object prioritization involves assigning a highest priority to audio objects that correspond to dialogue; and adding audio object prioritization metadata, based on the audio object prioritization, to the audio object metadata. 33. The apparatus of claim 32, wherein the interface system capable of receiving hearing environment data comprising a model of hearing loss and wherein the control system is capable of: adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization and the hearing environment data; and 

 rendering the audio objects into a plurality of speaker feed signals based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata, wherein each speaker feed signal corresponds to at least one of the reproduction speakers within the reproduction environment. 34. The apparatus of claim 32, wherein the interface system capable of receiving hearing environment data and wherein the control system comprises at least one excitation approximation module capable of determining excitation data that include an excitation for each of the plurality of audio objects, wherein an excitation is a function of a distribution of energy along a basilar membrane of a human ear and wherein at least one of the excitations is based, at least in part, on hearing environment data. 35. The apparatus of claim 34, wherein the control system includes a gain solver capable of receiving the excitation data and of determining gain data based, at least in part, on the excitations, the audio object prioritization and the hearing environment data. 36. The apparatus of claim 34 or 35, wherein the hearing environment data comprises at least one type of data selected from a group of data types consisting of environmental noise data, speaker deficiency data and hearing loss performance data. 37. A non-transitory medium having software stored thereon, the software including instructions for controlling at least one device for: receiving audio data comprising a plurality of audio objects, the audio objects including audio signals and associated audio object metadata; extracting one or more features from the audio data; determining an audio object type based, at least in part, on features extracted from the audio signals, wherein the audio object type is selected from a list of audio object types that includes dialogue; making an audio object prioritization based, at least in part, on the audio object type, wherein the audio object prioritization determines, at least in part, a gain to be applied during a process of rendering the audio objects into speaker feed signals and wherein making the audio object prioritization involves assigning a highest priority to audio objects that correspond to dialogue; and adding audio object prioritization metadata, based on the audio object prioritization, to the audio object metadata. 38. The non-transitory medium of claim 37, wherein the software includes instructions for controlling the at least one device for: receiving hearing environment data comprising a model of hearing loss; adjusting audio object levels according to the audio object prioritization and the hearing environment data; and rendering the audio objects into a plurality of speaker feed signals based, at least in part, on the audio object position metadata, wherein each speaker feed signal corresponds to at least one of the reproduction speakers within the reproduction environment.
